campus_name: System-level UPSOM/UPMC source — Western Psychiatric site not separately enumerated
source_system: Pitt SOM Office of Student Affairs + Pitt SOM Office of Medical Student Research and International Studies
opportunity_title: Visiting Medical Student Clinical Elective (UPSOM Domestic and International Programs)
specialty: Multi-specialty (incl. Psychiatry with USMLE Step 2 prerequisite)
clinical_exposure_level: Direct patient care under supervision (clinical elective)
audience_detail: Domestic: LCME/AOA accredited North America only. International: enrolled international students in final year only — graduates excluded.
audience_restriction_summary: Domestic = LCME/AOA NA only | International = enrolled final-year only (no graduates) | Site-specific Western Psychiatric placement not guaranteed
audience_public_caveat: Domestic visiting program: LCME / AOA accredited home institutions in North America only. International visiting program: enrolled international students in their final year of medical education only — graduates excluded.
visa_public_caveat: B-1/B-2 visa documentation provided on acceptance — J-1 / H-1B sponsorship not stated on source.
source_quote_under_280: Students who have completed their core clinical training and will be in the fourth year of medical education at their LCME- or AOA- accredited home institution in North America may apply for an elective experience at the University of Pittsburgh School of Medicine.
public_summary_draft: Visiting clinical electives at UPMC are governed by UPSOM (system-level). Western Psychiatric site placement is not separately enumerated. Domestic = LCME/AOA NA only. International = enrolled final-year only ($4,500/elective; Step 2 required for psychiatric).
public_limitations: System-level source — Western Psychiatric site placement not separately enumerated | International program $4,500 fee per clinical elective | International program excludes graduates | USMLE Step 2 required for psychiatric elective | B-1/B-2 visa documentation only
must_not_claim: guaranteed Western Psychiatric rotation | hospital-approved USCEHub listing | apply through USCEHub | IMG-friendly pathway | Caribbean accessible (domestic program) | J-1 sponsorship | H-1B sponsorship | observership for graduates
reviewer_notes: Curator approval contingent on PNG capture before runtime generation. Mirrors CC Hillcrest SYSTEM_PAGE_NO_SITE_SPECIFIC_GUARANTEE pattern.

campus_name: System-level NYC H+H MOSAIC source — Lincoln site not separately guaranteed
source_system: NYC Health + Hospitals MOSAIC + Lincoln Emergency Medicine residency department
opportunity_type: Visiting elective (system-level) plus Lincoln EM site-specific MS3/4 rotation
specialty: Multi-specialty per MOSAIC (EM IM GI Peds Primary Care Psych OB-GYN Ophth + others) + EM specifically per Lincoln EM source
clinical_exposure_level: Direct patient care under supervision (4-week rotation; mentorship + simulation + didactics)
audience_detail: US LCME-accredited allopathic OR AOA-accredited osteopathic medical students only. URM encouraged. Lincoln site placement not separately guaranteed.
audience_restriction_summary: US LCME or AOA only | Caribbean / IMG explicitly excluded by accreditation language | Lincoln site placement not guaranteed
audience_public_caveat: Eligibility: U.S. LCME-accredited allopathic medical school or AOA-accredited osteopathic medical school only.
visa_public_caveat: N/A — US-only audience; visa policy not relevant per source.
source_quote_under_280: Medical students must be attending a U.S. accredited allopathic medical school (i.e., are enrolled in Medical Degree-granting programs) or osteopathic medical schools.
public_summary_draft: Lincoln is one of multiple sites within NYC H+H. Visiting medical-student access flows through the system-level MOSAIC Visiting Scholars Program (US LCME/AOA only; $2,000 + $2,000 stipend; PDF email application).
public_limitations: System-level MOSAIC source — Lincoln site placement not separately guaranteed | Caribbean / IMG explicitly excluded by accreditation language | Lincoln EM secondary source supports EM specialty rotation only
must_not_claim: open to IMGs | open to Caribbean students | guaranteed Lincoln placement | all specialties (EM source restricted to EM) | visa sponsorship | apply through USCEHub | hospital-approved USCEHub listing | USCEHub affiliation
reviewer_notes: Curator approval contingent on PNG capture before runtime generation. Mirrors CCF Mercy READY_PUBLIC_US_ONLY pattern. Lincoln EM source is supplementary not primary.
